html:
Exclusive to:

Celestial Fissures are naturally-spawning objects exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in Return of Them. They are found on the Lunar Island.

They produce both light and an Enlightenment-increasing aura depending on the Moon Cycle, from no light and aura at New Moon to a large light and 100 /minute at Full Moon.

The Celestial Altar can be built by assembling the altar components on top of a Celestial Fissure. A Celestial Tribute can also be placed in one. Both structures access the same portion of the Celestial Crafting Tab.

Trivia

  • Celestial Fissures were introduced in the Turn of Tides update.
  • Celestial Fissures can be thought of as the lunar counterparts to Nightmare Fissures. Both are holes in the ground that release more or less light depending on their respective cycles (Moon Cycle for Celestial Fissures, Nightmare Cycle for Nightmare Fissures), and that increase Enlightenment or Insanity respectively.
DSTDSRoGSWHam
plainText:
Exclusive to:
Celestial Fissure
Object Info
0 to 100/min
Renewable?
Cannot be destroyed
Repaired By:
Source
Lunar Island
Miscellaneous
"moon_fissure"
“My brain pulses with peace and terror.”
–Wilson, when examining a Celestial Fissure.
“Ew, I think it's whispering to me.”
–Willow, when examining a Celestial Fissure.
“Wolfgang is frighten, but happy.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Celestial Fissure.
“I see the light.”
–Wendy, when examining a Celestial Fissure.
“IT WHISPERS FORBIDDEN MOON KNOWLEDGE TO ME”
–WX-78, when examining a Celestial Fissure.
“This is not the same type of magic I've seen from Maxwell's fuel.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Celestial Fissure.
“Oddly inviting, isn't it?”
–Woodie, when examining a Celestial Fissure.
“It's not shadow magic.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Celestial Fissure.
“An eternal light shines fröm within!”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Celestial Fissure.
“It's trying to talk, we think!”
–Webber, when examining a Celestial Fissure.
“It's giving me goosebumps.”
–Warly, when examining a Celestial Fissure.
“Makes head say 'Wee-aow, Wee-aow'”
–Wormwood, when examining a Celestial Fissure.
“Hm, wonder if I could use this energy somehow.”
–Winona, when examining a Celestial Fissure.
“This magic makes my brain so soft and floaty!”
–Wortox, when examining a Celestial Fissure.
“Glurph... head feel funny.”
–Wurt, when examining a Celestial Fissure.
“Do you hear that whispering, Woby?”
–Walter, when examining a Celestial Fissure.
“The cracks in this place are starting to show.”
–Wilson, when examining a Celestial Fissure without light.
“Iunno, maybe we can jam stuff in it.”
–Willow, when examining a Celestial Fissure without light.
“Tiny ground is crack apart!”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Celestial Fissure without light.
“Even the earth is falling apart under the pressure.”
–Wendy, when examining a Celestial Fissure without light.
“MOON HOLE!”
–WX-78, when examining a Celestial Fissure without light.
“The moon cycles here are beginning to make a lot more sense.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Celestial Fissure without light.
“That's a crack in the ground, eh?”
–Woodie, when examining a Celestial Fissure without light.
“I've more important things to deal with than holes in the ground.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Celestial Fissure without light.
“But a crack in the earth.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Celestial Fissure without light.
“Don't step on a crack!”
–Webber, when examining a Celestial Fissure without light.
“It looks like it goes down forever.”
–Warly, when examining a Celestial Fissure without light.
“Hello? No one there”
–Wormwood, when examining a Celestial Fissure without light.
“Wonder how far down it goes.”
–Winona, when examining a Celestial Fissure without light.
“A deep, dark hole that knows no end!”
–Wortox, when examining a Celestial Fissure without light.
“ECHO! Echo! Echoooo...”
–Wurt, when examining a Celestial Fissure without light.
“Any monsters down there?”
–Walter, when examining a Celestial Fissure without light.
Celestial Fissures are naturally-spawning objects exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in Return of Them. They are found on the Lunar Island.
They produce both light and an Enlightenment-increasing aura depending on the Moon Cycle, from no light and aura at New Moon to a large light and 100 /minute at Full Moon.
The Celestial Altar can be built by assembling the altar components on top of a Celestial Fissure. A Celestial Tribute can also be placed in one. Both structures access the same portion of the Celestial Crafting Tab.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
Celestial Fissures were introduced in the Turn of Tides update.
Celestial Fissures can be thought of as the lunar counterparts to Nightmare Fissures. Both are holes in the ground that release more or less light depending on their respective cycles (Moon Cycle for Celestial Fissures, Nightmare Cycle for Nightmare Fissures), and that increase Enlightenment or Insanity respectively.

'''Celestial Fissures''' are naturally-spawning objects exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', introduced in ''[[Return of Them]]''. They are found on the [[Lunar Island]].

They produce both light and an [[Enlightenment]]-increasing aura depending on the [[Moon Cycle]], from no light and aura at New Moon to a large light and 100 {{Enlightenment}}/minute at Full Moon.

The [[Celestial Altar]] can be built by assembling the altar components on top of a Celestial Fissure. A [[Celestial Tribute]] can also be placed in one. Both structures access the same portion of the [[Celestial Tab|Celestial Crafting Tab]].

== {{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* Celestial Fissures were introduced in the ''[[Return of Them#Turn of Tides|Turn of Tides]]'' update.
* Celestial Fissures can be thought of as the lunar counterparts to [[Nightmare Fissures]]. Both are holes in the ground that release more or less light depending on their respective cycles ([[Moon Cycle]] for Celestial Fissures, [[Nightmare Cycle]] for [[Nightmare Fissures]]), and that increase Enlightenment or [[Insanity]] respectively.
